Finding Summary Template: Leads, opportunities, customers, invoices, and KPI reports are being assembled from conflicting CRM exports, finance files, and shadow spreadsheets, so the business lacks a governed source of truth for core management numbers.
Common Root Cause Themes: No approved system-of-record policy; fragmented tool ownership; spreadsheet workarounds; mismatched object and field design

Finding Summary Template: Some core objects have a defined source, but KPI reporting still depends on manual extracts, local spreadsheets, or inconsistent field mapping across CRM, finance, and service data.
Common Root Cause Themes: Incomplete source mapping; partial integration coverage; inconsistent field standards; weak lineage documentation

Finding Summary Template: The source architecture is mostly reliable, though documentation, cross-system reconciliation, and end-to-end lineage can be strengthened to protect reporting as complexity grows.
Common Root Cause Themes: Growth outpacing documentation; limited reconciliation discipline; unclear integration ownership

Finding Summary Template: Required fields, owners, dates, and stage or status updates are not being captured when work happens, so records are completed late or inconsistently and reporting loses credibility.
Common Root Cause Themes: Missing mandatory fields; weak stage-exit rules; no owner accountability; users updating records after the fact

Finding Summary Template: Basic capture standards exist, but teams are inconsistent in how quickly and fully they update pipeline, billing, or customer records across the month.
Common Root Cause Themes: Uneven manager enforcement; partial training; inconsistent field validation; weak month-to-month discipline

Finding Title: Capture discipline is strong but can be tightened
Finding Summary Template: Capture discipline is generally strong, though exception handling and compliance monitoring can be tightened in high-volume or high-risk workflow steps.
Common Root Cause Themes: Limited exception monitoring; control fatigue in busy teams; light-touch compliance review

Finding Summary Template: Duplicates, missing values, stale statuses, and inconsistent categories across accounts, deals, invoices, or service records are materially distorting funnel, forecast, billing, or customer reporting.
Common Root Cause Themes: No cleansing routine; weak validation rules; uncontrolled updates; poor master-data hygiene

Finding Title: Data quality is usable but inconsistent
Finding Summary Template: Core reporting is possible, but recurring completeness, freshness, or classification issues still force manual reconciliation and reduce confidence in management numbers.
Common Root Cause Themes: Reactive cleanup; no measurable quality thresholds; inconsistent stewardship by record type

Finding Title: Data quality is strong with targeted improvement opportunities
Finding Summary Template: Data quality is broadly reliable, though targeted controls around anomalies, master-data drift, and exception trends would improve resilience as volume grows.
Common Root Cause Themes: Limited anomaly monitoring; master-data ownership not fully matured; controls focused on cleanup more than prevention

Finding Title: Workflow visibility is broken in the data
Finding Summary Template: The data does not show the full lifecycle from lead to cash and ongoing customer delivery, leaving handoffs, delays, rework, and drop-off largely invisible.
Common Root Cause Themes: Workflow steps not translated into system fields or statuses; missing timestamps; handoff events not captured; teams bypassing official stages

Finding Summary Template: Major workflow stages are visible, but key blind spots remain in handoffs, aging, backlog, rework, or exception tracking across critical processes.
Common Root Cause Themes: Partial process mapping; incomplete timestamping; queue states not captured; inconsistent use of workflow fields

Finding Title: Workflow visibility is strong but can be made more analytical
Finding Summary Template: Workflow visibility is strong, though reporting can become more diagnostic by highlighting stalled work, abnormal cycle time, and stage-level risk.
Common Root Cause Themes: Summary views dominate; limited exception design; weak comparative cycle-time analytics

Finding Summary Template: Core KPIs are not governed consistently: definitions, formulas, targets, or owners differ across dashboards, spreadsheets, and team reports, making management reporting unreliable.
Common Root Cause Themes: No KPI dictionary; inconsistent numerator/denominator logic; unclear ownership; thresholds not agreed

Finding Summary Template: A core KPI set exists, but some metrics still use inconsistent logic, thresholds, time periods, or ownership across reports and teams.
Common Root Cause Themes: Incomplete metric governance; weak alignment between team reports and leadership packs; partial target-setting discipline

Finding Title: KPI framework is strong with room for refinement
Finding Summary Template: KPI logic is largely controlled, though forward-looking indicators, threshold design, and benchmarking can be improved to make the layer more actionable.
Common Root Cause Themes: Focus on lagging indicators; limited benchmark context; thresholds not always tied to action

Finding Title: Reporting is manual and not decision-ready
Finding Summary Template: Leadership reporting depends on manual manipulation, duplicated reports, or poorly structured dashboards, so decisions are being made from delayed or conflicting information.
Common Root Cause Themes: Poor report design; weak data trust; version sprawl; no clear management-report standard

Finding Summary Template: Reporting is useful in places, but remains uneven in timeliness, consistency, and decision-focus across stakeholder groups.
Common Root Cause Themes: Report sprawl; weak refresh discipline; dashboards built for visibility rather than decisions; manual preparation burden

Finding Title: Reporting is strong but can be made more actionable
Finding Summary Template: Reporting is broadly trusted, though it can be made more action-led through clearer exception views, role-based layouts, and early-warning signals.
Common Root Cause Themes: Limited management-by-exception design; weak proactive alerting; summary-heavy dashboard structure

Finding Summary Template: No robust governance model exists to keep data quality, KPI logic, and reporting accuracy under control, with weak ownership and no formal correction routine.
Common Root Cause Themes: No governance model; unclear accountability; undocumented operating rules; issues resolved informally

Finding Title: Governance exists but is inconsistently applied
Finding Summary Template: Governance exists in parts, but accountability, review cadence, change control, and enforcement are inconsistent across teams.
Common Root Cause Themes: Partial SOP adoption; inconsistent leadership follow-through; governance meetings not linked to action tracking

Finding Title: Governance is strong with maturity improvement potential
Finding Summary Template: Governance is largely in place, though escalation, control tracking, and continuous improvement can be strengthened.
Common Root Cause Themes: Limited escalation discipline; control logs not consistently used; governance focused on maintenance more than improvement
